108.status: Exited in the fifth inning against the Rockies on June 3. Has been dealing with a groin/hip issue the last few weeks.
Ran at light intensity, took grounders and participated in shuffling drills to test ankle on June 1. Progressed to hitting in cage on June 2.
Participated in light on-field activity on June 1 and June 2 before progressing to running and hitting off a tee from the left side on June 3.
Was scratched roughly an hour before first pitch on June 1 and was held out as a precaution on June 2 and June 3, but said he was feeling better.
Left April 29 outing before the start of the third inning. Scheduled to start his throwing program in early June with a return date shortly after the All-Star break.
Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself while trying to beat out an infield single. Said he felt a pop and but has started to put pressure on his foot while wearing a boot.

Made four rehab appearances before being shut down. MRI came back clean May 15. Eligible to return from the 60-day IL May 26. Rehabbing at the club's complex in Arizona and resumed his throwing program.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Completed first bullpen session on May 30 at Camden Yards, throwing 15-20 pitches. Set to throw more bullpens week of June 1. Could start rehab assignment week of June 8.
Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Joined team in St. Petersburg, Fla., for series vs. Rays on May 18. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 29.
Exited May 7 start in Miami due to left forearm discomfort. Was examined by Dr. Keith Meister and received cortisone shot on May 19.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continuing to discuss treatment options. No clear timeline for return.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Expected to recover in time to return during the early part of the 2027 season.
Scratched from lineup ahead of May 12 game vs. the Yankees after feeling discomfort during pregame work. Underwent an MRI and was placed on IL the next day.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.

111.status: Took big step toward return by throwing side session at Fenway Park on June 2. Could make Minor League rehab outing in coming days, setting up a potential June 9 return.
Believed to be a nagging injury that shouldn't keep him out for long.
Underwent an MRI on his left lat that revealed "a very low grade" strain, according to interim manager Chad Tracy. Crochet will be able to resume throwing as soon as he is asymptomatic.
Returned to Fenway Park a week-and-a-half after surgery and will rehab under the supervision of the team's medical staff.
Threw live batting practice at Fenway Park on June 2, and is expected to start Minor League rehab assignment with one-inning stint on June 5.
Not progressing as hoped and will soon get checked out by team's medical staff.
Could do some light swinging toward the end of the the week, interim manager Chad Tracy said on May 27.
Will soon start forearm strengthening exercises with an eye toward throwing by mid-June.

111.status: Making 75 throws out to 105 feet three days a week, and 30 throws at 60 feet three days a week while throwing changeups those days. Targeting mound work in late June.

112.status: Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Iowa on June 2, playing center field and getting three ABs. Cubs want Shaw to play multiple games in a row (number of games TBD) prior to being activated.
Left knee meniscectomy (May 7). Logged 63 pitches in four innings (4 H, 3 R, 2 ER, 1 BB, 2 K) in a Minor League rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on May 31. Next rehab start is expected June 6.
Did a full warmup and simulated three innings in an extensive bullpen session on May 31 in St. Louis. On target to come off the IL to start during June 5-7 series vs. Giants.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 3 prospect, No. 87 overall)

Cleared to begin strengthening exercises on May 26.
Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.
Had setback (stress reaction near triceps/shoulder) after May 7 bullpen session, per manager Craig Counsell. Shut down for a month and moved rehab to Arizona.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

114.status: Set to begin rehab assignment on June 4 with Triple-A Columbus. Has been doing baseball activities. Initial timeline was four to eight weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on May 29.
MRI results confirmed inflammation but revealed no structural damage. Feeling better and working out, but no precise timeline yet for return.

115.status: Team announced his injury on June 1. No timeline for surgery or recovery at the moment.
Has thrown two bullpen sessions, and had one scheduled for June 2 at Anaheim. If all goes well, will face hitters at Coors Field on June 5 before going on a rehab assignment.
Beck said May 31 that he underwent an anti-inflammatory injection a couple of days earlier and is allowing it to take effect.
Reached 95 mph during his May 30 bullpen session. Scheduled for another during the team’s three-game road trip to Anaheim, and if all goes well, will face hitters when the team returns to Denver.
Limited to rest and recovery for an injury he sustained on May 12 and tried to play through until undergoing an MRI just before being placed on the IL.
On a regimen of rest and treatment. He and the club decided against attempting to accelerate healing with a PRP injection.
Doing treatment and some pool exercises after days of extreme discomfort.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 17 overall)
115.status: After receiving the results of multiple examinations, will undergo season-ending surgery, a source told MLB.com. Recovery period will last until late in the year.

Allowed two runs, two hits, two walks and struck out two batters in two-thirds of an inning in his first rehab start June 3 with Double-A Corpus Christi.
Went 2-for-5 as starting left field fielder in his seventh Minor League rehab game with Triple-A Sugar Land on June 3. He's 7-for-25 in his rehab stint.
Was in a 2-for-26 slump when he was placed on the IL.
Is hitting, throwing and catching bullpens and is close to a rehab assignment.

Scheduled to start June 4 for Triple-A Sugar Land at El Paso. Threw three innings, allowing one run and striking out five on 44 pitches, in his second Minor League rehab start on May 29.
Has begun a throwing progression. Was scratched before his scheduled start against the Twins in Minneapolis on May 19.
Has started a throwing progression. He first experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8 and was sent home to Houston to see doctors.

Originally expected to miss 6-8 weeks but is progressing more rapidly than expected. Began a Minor League rehab assignment on June 2; can return as soon as he proves capable of catching seven-plus innings on consecutive nights.

Made rehab starts on May 22 and 28, allowing four earned runs over seven combined innings. Will start again on June 3, but will need to show better results before the Mets will consider activating him.
Started baseball activities -- fielding drills, running sprints and swinging the bat in the indoor cages -- in late May but is not ready for a rehab assignment.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Was initially given a 4-8-week timeline but was still not throwing as of an early June update.
Timeline will remain fluid until symptoms subside. Was progressing better as of a May 29 update and could advance to baseball activities in the relatively near future.
Is expected to miss 2-3 weeks with a flexor strain suffered while running the bases in a May 25 game. Timetable will become more specific once he restarts baseball activities.

Fractured his leg on a comebacker May 15. Will need 6-8 weeks to heal and another six or so for a Spring Training-style build-up. That should take him well past the All-Star break in a best-case scenario.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks.

Scheduled to play seven innings with Triple-A Las Vegas on June 2. No timeline yet on how many rehab games he will need before returning.
Began a throwing progression program on June 1. No timetable for a return.
Shut down from throwing for a minimum of three weeks, beginning May 18. Received an ultrasound guided cortisone injection on May 19.

Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Experienced elbow soreness after game on May 27 and was diagnosed with a minor right forearm strain. Expected to spend minimal time on IL.

Suffered apparent injury on May 9 in San Francisco. Foot began swelling during flight to Pittsburgh on May 10. Cleared by doctor on May 29 to resume weight lifting activities in shoes. Will be reassessed during June 1-4 trip to Houston.
Resumed baseball activities on May 13; Rehabbing at team's training facilities in Bradenton, Fla. Playing catch on flat ground and is anticipated to throw a bullpen session at start of June.

Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Began playing catch in late May.
Expected to pitch again this season, after an MRI revealed a flexor strain. Began playing catch in late May. No timeline for a return to the mound.
Fouled a ball off his toe on May 5, sustaining what the team said was a small fracture. Making notable progress -- is swinging pain-free and catching bullpens.
Placed on the injured list one day after working two scoreless innings in relief at Milwaukee. Began a throwing program on May 23. Could return as a reliever.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery in April 2025. Began a rehab stint on May 7. Had his rehab transferred to Double-A San Antonio on May 17. Is building up as a starter.
Experienced concussion symptoms after a fastball off the chin on April 18. Initially cleared protocols, but symptoms arose in the days afterward. Has not resumed baseball activity.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

Targeting a rehab assignment in the next week.
Undergoing an MRI on May 29 to make sure everything is clear before ramping up his throwing program.

Will miss two to three weeks with the adductor strain.
Received a PRP injection on May 19 in San Francisco. Continuing to rehab in Arizona and began a hitting progression June 1.

Currently in a walking boot and will see Dr. Robert Anderson for a second opinion later this week.

Tracking toward a rehab assignment, with Oliver Marmol hopeful that Urias could begin that assignment over the coming weekend with Triple-A Memphis.

139.status: Removed for precautionary reasons on June 1 but said he felt "100%" ready to play, if needed. Did not start June 2-3 but was available off the bench. Team hoped June 4 off-day would allow him to fully recover and resume normal play June 5.

Felt stiffness in his wrist while playing catch on May 29 that didn't subside on May 30. Rather than pitching through discomfort, team decided to give him a few days off from throwing to heal. Resumed playing catch June 3.

Signed to a Major League contract on April 14. Began a rehab assignment with one-inning outings for the Rookie-level Florida Complex League Rays on May 23 and May 26. Threw two scoreless innings for Triple-A Durham on June 2.

After being designated for assignment on May 29 following an outing on May 27, Heasley was returned to the roster on June 2 due to an injury he sustained while active with the Rays and placed on the 15-day IL with no timeline for his return.

He reported soreness in back of right shoulder on April 15. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Had a setback while playing catch from 75 feet, so he underwent a PRP injection. "Going to be a while," manager Kevin Cash said.

Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8, with goal to return in June or July. Throwing bullpens and making progress, but no decision on when he will progress to facing hitters.
Felt soreness during recent series in New York and while pitching in Baltimore on May 25. Imaging revealed mild tendon inflammation, but should resume throwing around June 1.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Playing catch with the goal to start bullpen sessions on June 8.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury, had his rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle on April 11 then shut down May 12 due to inflammation in his left shoulder. Not taking part in baseball activities, so no timeline for return.
Scratched from May 16 lineup and placed on the IL one day later. Cash said he could require 6-8 weeks of recovery after surgery. Not taking part in workouts as of May 29.

Felt his hamstring “grab” while running to first base on an infield single May 22. Did not feel better running first to third and exited the game. MRI revealed a Grade 1 strain that will sideline him for 6-8 weeks.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

140.status: Threw a 20-pitch live batting practice session on June 3.

Played two games with Triple-A Round Rock on May 30-31 and continued his rehab assignment June 2 with Double-A Frisco, going 1-for-2 with two walks and a stolen base. Expected to return at the start of the next homestand.

Martin missed 30 games (April 15 to May 19) previously. He allowed four earned runs in four appearances (four innings) since returning, surrendering seven hits and two walks without recording a strikeout.

Underwent surgery on May 20. Cast has been removed and he has begun throwing, but he is not yet swinging a bat.

Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

Has felt discomfort -- off and on -- for the past five weeks, but it flared up while warming up on May 28. He will not throw for the next four weeks.

Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

Hopeful to beat eight-week recovery timeframe. Able to stand up and walk on his own. Sustained the injury during stretching in between innings on May 27.

147.injury: Bone bruise in right rib cage
147.status: Was out of lineup June 2 and 3 against Guardians and is awaiting results after a consultation with a specialist. Is able to perform weight room exercises.
Took live batting practice vs. Angel Chivilli on June 3 at Yankee Stadium. Could begin Minor League rehab assignment June 5.
Tossed live batting practice to Jasson Domínguez and Giancarlo Stanton on June 3 at Yankee Stadium. Scheduled to toss to Stanton again on June 6.

Threw June 1 and 2 from 75 feet and "seems to be responding to everything," manager Aaron Boone said. Will continue to ramp up throwing program, aiming for 90 feet-plus. Set to be re-imaged week of June 7.
Took live batting practice on June 3, facing Angel Chivilli, and is scheduled to do so again June 6. Has been performing agility exercises and running lightly.
Most recent mound session was 20 pitches as of June 2 and said he is “feeling great.” Could face hitters by the end of June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.
Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Will not throw for at least three weeks.

158.status: Returned from the IL on May 31 only to feel discomfort during a pregame throwing session and land back on the IL. MRI scan showed trap strain. Will see a specialist to further pinpoint a diagnosis. (last updated: June 3)
Threw 42 pitches while simulating three innings in a live batting practice session on June 3. The Brewers planned to discuss next steps with Woodruff following the outing. (last updated: June 3)
Took live at-bats against rehabbing Brewers starter Brandon Woodruff on June 3 and is nearing a rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville, manager Pat Murphy said. (last updated: June 3)

Threw 3 2/3 innings "without issue" in his first start in the rookie-level Arizona Complex League on June 2. The Brewers moved him to the ACL to take advantage of the league's re-entry rule if needed. (last updated: June 3)
Received a facet injection in his back on June 2 to relieve inflammation. (last updated: June 3)
Threw eight of 10 pitches for strikes for Triple-A Nashville on May 29 in the first of what was expected to be a multi-outing rehab assignment. (last updated: May 30)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 12)
